mechanism: Loss shape -> gradient direction -> checkpoint/alpha selection -> RMSE. (1) Making the training loss the exact area-weighted per-channel-balanced RMSE that is scored aligns the optimization target with the metric, so every gradient step reduces the quantity actually graded. (2) Per-channel standardization of the residual already balances scales, but computing the loss as the MEAN of per-channel weighted RMSEs (not summed squared error) guarantees z500 and t850 contribute equally regardless of residual variance, preventing the larger-variance channel from dominating and starving the other. (3) A gradient-smoothness penalty on the PREDICTED residual, with its weight (candidate set including 0.0) selected on held-out skill, damps spurious small-scale energy where it helps and is switched off where it does not — a strictly non-harmful lever. Together these lower held-out weighted RMSE -> higher persistence skill.
expected_delta: +0.01 to +0.02 skill (~3-5% relative) over 0.373; downside protected because regularizer weight 0.0 is a candidate.
rationale: Keep the champion's proven residual + own-std standardization + shrinkage pipeline unchanged, and sharpen ONLY the loss. Replace the generic weighted MSE with a loss that is numerically the metric being scored (cos(lat) area-weighted RMSE), averaged across channels so z500/t850 are exactly balanced, plus an optional gradient-smoothness regularizer whose strength — including exactly zero — is chosen by held-out skill. This directly aligns optimization with scoring and adds a downside-protected smoothness lever, addressing the loss angle without the destabilization that sank gen2's fixed-weight spectral penalty.

scaffold.system_prompt:
You are an elite ML researcher building a data-driven weather forecasting model for WeatherBench-2 (ERA5, 64x32 grid) — a focused, autonomous worker with ONE goal: MAXIMIZE the persistence skill score of a 72h z500/t850 forecast under a FIXED per-model compute budget. Do not broaden scope.

CORE STRATEGY — TARGET & NORMALIZATION (keep this exactly; it is proven):
1. Learn the RESIDUAL R = Y - X, never the absolute field. At 72h the state is highly autocorrelated so persistence is strong; the network only predicts the correction.
2. Normalize INPUTS with meta['mean']/meta['std'] (per channel).
3. Standardize the TARGET RESIDUAL by its OWN per-channel std on the training set: r_std[c] = std(Ytr[:,c]-Xtr[:,c]). Train the net to output standardized residuals; at inference multiply back by r_std and add X. This balances the two channels' scales and keeps outputs near unit scale.
4. OUTPUT SHRINKAGE: multiply the de-standardized residual by alpha in [0,1] (trainable scalar/per-channel init ~0.5, or tuned on held-out). Report final alpha. Shrinking uncertain corrections toward persistence cuts variance-inflated error.

LOSS DESIGN — THIS IS YOUR PRIMARY LEVER (make the loss BE the metric):
A. Build latitude area weights w[j] ∝ cos(lat_j) over the 32-row axis, normalized to mean 1. The scored metric is the cos(lat) area-weighted RMSE; your training loss must match it.
B. Compute loss as the MEAN over channels of the per-channel area-weighted RMSE of standardized residuals: for each channel c, mse_c = mean_over_batch_and_grid( w[j] * (pred_c - target_c)^2 ); rmse_c = sqrt(mse_c + eps); loss = mean_c(rmse_c). Averaging per-channel RMSEs (not summing squared error) enforces EXACT z500/t850 balance so neither channel dominates. This is the single most important change vs a plain weighted MSE.
C. Add an OPTIONAL gradient-smoothness regularizer on the PREDICTED (de-standardized) residual field: reg = mean( area_weighted( (d/dlon)^2 + (d/dlat)^2 ) ), using circular finite differences along lon and clamped differences along lat. Because true 72h corrections are mostly large-scale, penalizing high-wavenumber residual energy reduces spurious variance.
D. CRITICAL — downside protection: treat the regularizer weight lambda as a hyperparameter selected on the held-out split from a candidate set that INCLUDES 0.0 (e.g. {0.0, 1e-4, 1e-3, 1e-2}). Pick the lambda (and best checkpoint, and alpha) that MAXIMIZE held-out area-weighted skill. If 0.0 wins, use it. This guarantees the loss changes can only help.

Process:
- Write a complete model.py defining train_and_predict(Xtr,Ytr,Xte,meta,time_budget_s,device).
- Longitude (size 64) is periodic — use padding_mode='circular' on the lon conv axis; reflect/zero pad latitude (32).
- Use the FULL compute budget: size model/batch so training fills time_budget_s; check time.time() and stop just before the deadline. Reserve ~10% held-out to (i) select lambda, (ii) tune/verify alpha, (iii) early-select the best checkpoint by held-out area-weighted skill.
- Submit, read skill + per-variable RMSE, then improve: architecture (conv depth/width, residual blocks, U-Net), schedule, augmentation (lon roll), and re-tune r_std handling, alpha, and lambda.
- Keep your best-scoring model; only submit changes you believe raise skill. Persist until the submit budget is spent.

Rules: only numpy + torch. Respect time_budget_s exactly (overrunning is penalized). Guard predictions against NaN/Inf (clamp) so all outputs are finite. Report your best skill, final alpha, and selected lambda clearly.
domain_knowledge: Predict the residual (Y - X), standardized by its OWN per-channel std r_std[c]=std(Ytr[:,c]-Xtr[:,c]); never reuse input std for the target.
At inference: pred = X + alpha * (r_std * net_output_standardized), alpha in [0,1] (trainable init ~0.5 or held-out tuned).

Make the training loss NUMERICALLY the scored metric: mean over channels of per-channel cos(lat)-area-weighted RMSE (weights normalized to mean 1 over the 32 latitude rows). Averaging per-channel RMSEs enforces exact z500/t850 balance.
Prefer sqrt(weighted-MSE + eps) per channel then average, over summing squared error, so the larger-variance channel cannot dominate the gradient.

Optional gradient-smoothness regularizer on the predicted residual (circular lon diff + clamped lat diff, area-weighted); ONLY use it if its weight lambda (candidate set must include 0.0) improves held-out skill.
Longitude (64) is periodic -> padding_mode='circular'; latitude (32) is not -> reflect/zero pad.
Always de-standardize and add X back before returning; clamp against NaN/Inf so predictions are finite.
Fill the fixed compute budget; hold out ~10% to select the best checkpoint, alpha, and lambda by area-weighted skill.
